videos.transcript: One of the most beautiful and popular destinations in the world is Goa. Goa has everything a tourist needs. Goa has everything Obviously, this is the editor's most favorite destination But I don't know why this year, Goa got the evil eye of Soros. Empty beaches, missing Goa's buzz, and news of bloodshed. Tourist from Andhra Pradesh Bolla Ravi Teja went to dine in North Goa the owner asked for extra money, since it was post midnight order this led to argument and one worker at the restaurant beat Bolla so badly that he died on the spot The political leaders of Goa still say that everything is fine there. But in the last 5 years, a lot has changed in Goa. Nature, beaches, gambling, nightlife, but the number of tourists has become very less. Goa is still trying to reach the 2019 levels. And the major reason behind this is that the recurring tourists from other countries who considered Goa as their second home changed the address of their second home. When this was highlighted by Ramanuj Mukherjee on X, the government immediately took action. They took action and filed a case against Ramanuj Mukherjee. The government also tried to show the media that everything is fine in Goa. Beaches are full. We have left the expectation of improvement due to criticism. But now, due to criticism, an FIR is being filed. And that too for a small, tourism related issue. The situation has come to a point where you cannot talk openly about tourism. It is clear that there is a problem in Goa tourism. If this was not the case, then there would not be a need to press the voice. People would laugh at such claims and move ahead But in Goa, the fraud that is happening with the tourists, these are content materials for Instagrammers To understand Goa's problem we have to understand Goa's target audience Countries where winter becomes unbearable, their people search for destinations, which is close to nature, pocket friendly and tourist friendly and Goa fulfilled those criteria and tourists came in numbers but what happened after Covid that tourists stopped coming? Goa's competition has become stiff now - Thailand, Sri Lanka, Central Asia which is giving better experience at low price international tourists are looking at cost and convenience and factor of free visa on arrival, people are finding it convenient and Goa could control few things, like behavior with tourists taxi mafia, cesses, tourist unfriendly Govt policies which have led to empty beaches Goa has become a good example of this pan India problem, when it comes to tourism And don't go on social media influencers. Look at the figures. Government of India has said that 100 million foreign tourists will come to India by 2047. These days, there is no planning for 2-3-4 years. The planning is for 2047. So 100 million tourists by 2047. Is it a big number? In 2019, 90 million tourists went to France. In 2023, 85 million tourists went to Spain If India has to bring 100 million tourists by 2047, then we will have to show 15% annual growth rate in tourists. So is it possible or not? Let's understand this. Our golden period for tourist arrivals was 2001-2019 at that time we were doing 8.5% and now need is 15% But the truth is that we are de-growing In 2019, we had a peak in tourism with 10.9 million tourists. if we compare this to a city like Dubai it is a little shameful, as it attracted 16.7 million tourists and that number is growing, first half of 2024 Dubai attracted more visitors than same period in 2019 Dubai is growing 10-11% and India is degrowing by 10%. So what has happened in the last 4-5 years that foreign tourists have chosen other destinations over Goa, or India? Not only Goa, why is the Indian tourism industry struggling overall? What are the things that have stolen the smile of Indian tourists? Let's understand in today's episode with solutions. Cyber fraud is one of the biggest issues in our country. We have made an entire episode on this. Do watch it if you get time. But foreign tourists don't know about all this. They book a hotel, reach Goa, and find out that they have been frauded Some hotels are fake listed, fake websites are made, and foreign tourists who are not familiar with regular booking websites, get trapped There are other scams that are going on in the name of hotel booking. The government is working on this, trying to warn people. Don't share OTP, check the authenticity of websites. Criminal will do crime, why don't you beware we will be cautious but someone in Russia won't know this some scams are run by hotels themselves, at entry or exit charges are imposed upon reaching the hotel. Many times hotels overbook the guests. Misleading advertisements are made. Online fake ratings are put up. Something is shown, the room is different Free upgrades are given during check-in. Tourists are also very happy. During check-out, it is said that nothing is free in this world. Give money. If all these issues will be there, then why will the tourists come back? Along with this, in today's date, all these things are shared in online reviews, experiences, etc. The reputation of Goa is ruined. And why do people fall for these fraud ads and cybercrime? Because you won't find decent hotels in genuine hotels, websites and popular travel portals at a reasonable price. Not at all during the season. For example, we compared. Marriott, one of the biggest hotel chains in the world, Courtyard by Marriott in North Goa was showing room tariff of Rs 15,000 Courtyard by Marriott in Pattaya, Thailand was showing tariff of Rs 9,300 including taxes Hotel prices fluctuate season by season But in general, due to irregular prices and high prices during the season Tourism is suffering in the state and Goa is being defamed Well, if it was just high hotel prices, the problem wouldn't be so big. But the problem is even worse. You land in Goa, but the local taxi driver is smiling more than you. Well, we got another new goat. Usually, as soon as we leave any airport, we book Uber, Ola, Rapido, etc But this is not possible in Goa. Here the system is Pro Max. The taxi mafia of Goa doesn't let any app-based service provider run. You can use as much technology as you want. They rule here As soon as you land at the airport in Goa, the fare that the taxi driver gets, you have to pay three or four times more than that. So, the first experience of coming to Goa is ruined there. And along with that, there is no public transport. There is no cheap travel in Goa. No metro, no bus network Yes, there is a cheap alternative that you can rent a vehicle. But for those who don't know how to drive, this option is not there And renting a car can be done when you are travelling in a group and the cost is split. An app called Goa Miles was launched by Goa Tourism, but you will say that IRCTC app is better after looking at its interface taxi prices are not controlled and Govt is not benefiting either Govt generates 10 crore rupees revenue, but the taxis generate 100 crore rupees. But because of unpaid GST and TDS the taxi operators don't pay. The group of taxi operators is so strong that even the politicians say, let it run as it is. But the tourist destination that does not even have a reliable public transport compete with the world's biggest tourist destinations? Think for yourself. It is also true that although Goa attracts worldwide tourists, the majority of tourists come from Israel and Russia, and there is a war going on in the two places. But if geopolitical factors were bringing such a big dent in tourism and this number was visible, then it was necessary that the government should introduce better packages. to attract other countries' tourists where there is peace They come to Goa compared to other destinations, like Visa on arrival facility which Thailand and Sri Lanka are providing we give it a political spin that they gave us visa on arrival we became world leader they just want your money, you are leaving your country and going to their country for vacation, you are going to Dubai, Sri Lanka, Thailand, then take a visa on arrival for free. Spend money here. And if you look at India, the normal e-visa application is also called complicated. Many foreigners who want to come to India, they have repeatedly reported that the visa process here is not smooth. There are delays in getting a visa, US and Europe travelers are questioned a lot when there is so much hassle who would want to travel along with this other countries are providing better value for money once Goa used to be cheap, now GST, expensive tariff, bad public transport have changed it Influencers are posting from foreign destinations they are telling how they are enjoying at low prices without hassle So obviously, more people are getting attracted to it. A few years ago, a report came out which said that in Goa around 250 people have died in the last 12 years This figure is quite high. And the maximum crimes are committed by the Goa Drug Mafia. Now, Goa DGP Jaspal Singh says that there is no Drug Mafia in Goa. So, a question. Where is this drug haul coming from? Are rats spitting it or distributing it? We don't know. Even the editor knows the truth. I don't know why the police is denying it On one hand, DGP says there is no mafia. On the other hand news of this or that consignment caught On the way, a tourist is suddenly sold drugs openly in the crowded areas of Goa. This is a situation that many tourists are facing now And where there is such a drug cartel, where there is such a drug supply, it is normal for law and order to be weak there. Crime is being committed under the guise of drugs. And here, drugs are seized no one solves the problems that are coming again and again. This problem has not been solved from the root. Be it this government or the previous government, all have been unsuccessful. Hotel scam, expensive transport, someone can bear it once, but the place where the law and order is the problem, becomes an issue of safety, there will not be a repeat customer, repeat tourist, you can take this into consideration And as we said at the beginning of the episode, the latest New Year case, where a tourist is killed over dinner But the Goa Chief Minister's statement repeatedly says that there is no problem, no law and order issue, no drug mafia, no lack of tourists So let's stay in denial Foreign tourists are decreasing, that's true.. but Indian tourists are considering Goa so some balancing out happened But it is possible that in the future, these people will also decrease a tourist shared Goa experience on X.. they shared why Goa is declining in tourism and why international destinations are growing in popularity one observation - the attitude of locals towards tourists unwelcoming and hostile attitude In response, a local entrepreneur from Goa replied, not sorry or that we will do better... He said, we don't want any tourists, get out, period. It is true that many Indian tourists going to Goa are drunk driving, fighting, making foreigners uncomfortable but we don't want tourists, this can't be a solution. If the government is interested in solutions, not in FIR and blame game, they have to reestablish safe reputation of Goa one message to tourists, come and enjoy, within the law. Party responsibly. Govt has to decide what will Goa be known for for cheap product, or for adventure, culture pick one specialty.. how many tourists can you bring by just showing sun and sand make a specialty, work on public transport if not metro, then buses if you cannot end taxi mafia then forget that Goa will be able to compete with international destinations 1 way traffic in crowded tourist zone, beach cleaning initiatives, segregating, transporting and disposing of garbage, tourism promotion. the government will not agree to this, but Goa has reached its peak without the government's contribution. Now think, if the government's contribution comes, then the Vegas of India can give a good beating to others but the government's support will have to come at the right time and in the right meaning. What do you think? If you have an option between Sri Lanka, Thailand, Central Asia and Goa, where would you go? Write in the comment section, what will be your destination? Where will it be? Why? Maybe we are thinking wrong, or maybe you can give us more information on this, what things can we fix?

videos.transcript: South Goa part of Goa that has a lot of hidden gems waiting to be discovered if you ask a Goa regular whether he or she likes North Goa or South Goa there is 90 percentage probability that they will say South Goa in fact people who visit South Goa are the ones who want to see something new experience the authentic part of Goa or just have a relaxed time away from the crowd in today's video we will be covering such 10 hidden jumps of Goa that you must visit hey guys welcome back to security cubes and this is Sagar this is the third video of my Goa series if you haven't watched my previous videos then do watch it after this video we have covered a lot of details including how to reach Goa places to stay where to eat what to see and the total budget in those videos the links to the videos would be provided in the description box below in today's video we will concentrate only on South Goa let's jump into our list of top 10 places to visit in South Goa [Music] thank you Kola Beach is probably the most beautiful beach you can visit in Goa reaching Kola Beach is a little challenging task the roads leading to the beach is not in good condition especially the last one kilometer stretch is quite an effort you can cover it on a two wheeler with good riding skills or if you have got an SUV then you can drive it all the way to the last Point otherwise you will have to park your vehicle and walk for around 20 to 30 minutes to reach the beach because Kola beaches are known to a lot of people and since it is quite difficult to reach this beach you can expect less crowd here the beach is undoubtedly one of the best beaches in Goa it is very calm and peaceful the major attraction of Kola Beach is its lagoons you can find fresh water streams surrounded by tall coconut trees on either side kayaking in the shallow water is a popular activity here I definitely recommend you to try kayaking here the food and say options at Kola Beach is limited if you are visiting it in the off season like in summers or monsoon then they may even be closed so carry food and water accordingly [Music] the best beach in Goa is the one which you visit accidentally that's how we discovered this gem called kabadrama Beach we were going to Cabo drama Fort and due to navigation error we ended up at kabadrama beach when we had a look at the beach from the cliff we were awestruck by its beauty the Blue Ocean and the surrounding Greenery made us speechless the kabadrama beach has lagoons just like Kola Beach the water in the lagoon is crystal clear and we saw hundreds if not thousands of black Seasons we were lucky to be the only ones here when we arrived the beach was very clean and the perfect spot to get into the water as well there is a very popular restaurant called the cape Goa on this beach we had lunch here and totally loved this place the food Ambience and Views were incredible [Music] butterfly Beach is a tiny little beach situated between agonda and palolam this is a remote beach and there are only two ways to reach it you can either take a boat ride or do a shot trekking to reach this beach taking a boat ride is the easiest way to reach butterfly Beach you'll get boats from palolin beach agonda beach and Putnam Beach and the cost of the boat ride can range between 500 Rupees to 700 rupees per person depending on your bargaining skills if you are feeling little adventurous then you can also do kayaking all the way from palolam Beach to Butterfly Beach it is a three kilometer stretch and kayaking in open ocean is going to be crazy experience if you are choosing the second option which is hiking then you will have to hike anywhere between 30 minutes to 60 Minutes depending on whether you are on a two wheeler or four wheeler the roads say not as you drive towards butterfly Beach the two wheelers can go a little further as compared to four wheelers and at one point of time you will have to park your vehicle and start walking if you are on a four wheeler then I suggest you to take a boat ride instead of a long hike the only downside of taking a boat ride is that you get just 30 to 40 minutes to explore the place it should be sufficient for most people if you're looking for a laid back time at butterfly Beach then better choose hiking the beach is small with rocks on either side and a white sandy beach in the middle you can climb on the rocks to get a better view of the beach there are no shops or eateries on this beach so carry water and food accordingly especially if you have decided to Trek butterfly Beach used to be one of the most beautiful beaches but not anymore due to over tourism this place is filled with trash all around irresponsible tourists have later the place and it has lost its charm if you are visiting butterfly Beach please make sure not to litter the place next to Butterfly Beach is Honeymoon Beach but it is not accessible by foot the only option to reach it is by boat ride if you're taking boat ride then they will show you Honeymoon Beach from far but they won't allow you to get down there [Music] if you want to explore Goa Beyond beaches then you must visit netra Valley Wildlife Sanctuary it is around 30 kilometers from Palo Alam and 44 kilometers from madgaon netravi is a protected Forest range maintained by Goa Forest Department this is strictly a no plastic Zone hence it is not allowed to carry Senegalese plastic or alcohol inside the forest there are two main attractions inside netrauli Sanctuary mainappi waterfalls and savory waterfalls to reach minority waterfalls you will have to do a long Trek through dense forest the track distance is 4 kilometer and it will take around one to one and a half hour the water force is surrounded by lush green forest and it looks stunning especially in Monsoon since pineapple demands long hike very less people visit this place if you are someone who likes less crowded places then this would be the perfect place for you there is a natural pool at the base of the waterfalls and it has shallow depth hence it's a perfect place if you want to get into the water the next waterfalls is Savory waterfalls reaching this Falls is easier than minor P it is just one kilometer walk which would take less than 20 minutes since the hike is easier it can get crowded during pcas the waterfalls is equally stunning and it is totally worth wasting Saudi waterfalls the natural pool is deeper here so it is advised to stay behind the safety points no matter whether you know swimming or not there is a changing room facility also available so if you are thinking to get into the water then don't hesitate date hats off to the forest department for not just opening up these attractions for tourists but also maintaining them really well if you are wondering whether it is safe to visit these places then don't worry it is completely safe there are signboards everywhere and there will be security persons near the waterfalls as well the only issue that we faced was there was no mobile network inside the forest range luckily we had the offline maps downloaded so if you are visiting netrauli then don't forget to download offline maps there are no restaurants or shops inside the forest so do carry sufficient water and food apart from these two waterfalls there are few smaller waterfalls like baman Buddha waterfalls and pooper waterfalls if you have time and energy then you can visit them on the way back [Music] foreign [Music] Wildlife Sanctuary you must also include bubbling Lake also called the budwood lake on your itinerary if you are hearing it for the first time the name of this place might feel strange and quite funny just to clarify one thing even though it is called bubbling Lake technically it is not a lake it's just a small pond but a mysterious one if you pay close attention to the water start noticing bubbles coming from the bottom of the pond that's why it is named bubbling Lake the bubbles are formed at random location at random frequency some people say clapping or shotting increases the frequency of bubbles but I found it has nothing to do with the sound it's just random even though there are many scientific theories behind this phenomena the exact reason why bubbles are formed Still Remains a mystery you had to visit this place personally to experience this wonder the water in the pond is very clean and you see a lot of small fishes in it next to this point is a Temple dedicated to Lord gopinath the place is surrounded by Greenery and it is very calm and peaceful not many tourists visit this place so it is a great spot if you are looking for an offbeat place to explore [Music] kabadrama Fort is a small Fort surrounded by ocean on three sides there are mainly three important spots inside the fort that are worth the visit the first one is the Fort pay point from here you can get an incredible view of the ocean next is Saint Anthony's Chapel this Chapel is small and beautiful it is still used by locals for doing prayers the last and the best part is the Pebble Beach to reach Pebble Beach you'll have to walk down for about 5 minutes the trail is easy and needs almost no effort to reach the beach this place is called Pebble Beach because there are thousands of Pebbles here the beach is not suitable for swimming but the beauty of this place is incredible apart from these three spots there is also a sea cave at this place but to reach it you will have to Trek down for about 20 to 30 minutes if you are someone who likes to explore sea caves then definitely check it out [Music] Beach is probably the cleanest Beach you'll find in entire Goa in fact it is a very special Beach because it is a nesting ground of sea turtles yes that's why it is popularly known as Turtle Beach by the locals these sea turtles are called Olive Ridley Turtles and they come to this beach to lay their eggs the government has taken all the measures to protect the nesting grounds that's why you won't find any commercial activities on this beach there are no shacks no vehicles and no fishing on this beach is super clean with white sandy beach and Crystal Clear Water the nesting season will be usually in the month of Jan and Feb and the eggs hatch within 40 to 45 days so if you are visiting Goa between Chan to April then a visit to kalikibaga is a must we wasted Goa in the month of October which is not a nesting season still we totally fell in love with this place the calmness and beauty of galgibaga were to a whole new level not just galkibaga there are few more beaches like morissim Beach and agonda Beach that are also nesting sites of these sea turtles foreign Beach is a beautiful Sandeep beach with clear water and laid back atmosphere this beach has a lot of shacks and it is one of the busiest beaches in South Goa there are many water sports activities you can do here among which kayaking and boating are the most popular ones we can do kayaking in open ocean which is going to be a thrilling experience I suggest you do kayaking in the evening so that you can watch the mesmerizing Sunset while you are in the middle of the sea the boats will usually take you on an R right and show you Honeymoon Beach butterfly Beach Turtle Rock and dolphin sightseeing palolium is a great place for shopping too there are so many shops near the beach the prices are bit expensive as compared to other places in Goa but the collections were pretty good palolame has a lot of good cafes restaurants and pubs it is a great place to stay as well it is a hotspot for many Travelers and it is very convenient to reach other tourist attractions from this place I have given the recommended stay options in Goa are in the description box below do check it out after watching this video [Music] if you are looking for some Adventure then you should visit dosagar waterfalls no doubt those other waterfalls is one of the most beautiful waterfalls in India it is situated in bhagwan mahavir wildlife sanctuary if you look at the map it is located close to the Karnataka and Goa border there are two ways to reach dosagar waterfalls the first is trekking along the railway track this track either starts from Castle Rock railway station or coulomb railway station we had done this Trek back in 2014 where we Trek from Castle Rock to those other waterfalls but this Trek is banned now I still see a lot of people doing this Trek but I don't suggest you to try it the second and the best method to reach those other waterfalls is by taking a Jeep ride you will get the Jeeps from a place called kulam around 36 kilometer from madgaon private vehicles are not allowed to enter the forest so you will have to park the vehicle here and buy the tickets for the Jeep ride the price of a single Jeep is 3500 rupees which can accommodate up to 7 people the exact price per head can vary depending on the number of people in the Jeep in addition to this you will have to rent out a life jacket for 40 rupees and pay another 100 rupees as a forest entry charges this off-road Jeep ride is 10 kilometer long and would take close to 30 minutes you will have to walk another 20 minutes to finally reach the waterfalls you get just one and a half hour to enjoy the waterfalls including the trekking time the moment you see the waterfalls you know it was worth the effort the pool is quite deep here so make sure to wear life jacket at all times whether you know swimming or not since do Sagar is very famous it can get crowded during pcas so I would suggest you to be here as early as possible the best time to visit do Sagar is in Monsoon and if you're visiting in rainy season make sure to wear shoes with good grip as the trail can be slippery there are a lot of monkeys in this area so don't carry any food items when you are here [Music] before we talk about the last destination on our list if this video is helpful then hit on that like button and if you are new to this channel then please subscribe to sucker cubes back to the topic next we have salilim Dam if you're wishing Goa in the monsoon time then a visit to salalim dam is a must this specialty of this dam is the spillway the Overflow water goes through a U-shaped Spillway The View during this time would be mesmerizing there is a similar time in Karnataka also called chikliola Reservoir that also has a seamless Spillway design there is a botanical garden and Eco park next to this stamp so if you are coming with kids then they will enjoy it for sure that is my list of top 10 places to visit in South Goa let me know which is your favorite in the comment section below Goa is filled with hundreds of such hidden spots if you know any such places that deserves to be on this list then do let me know in the comment section as well if you're looking for places to visit in North Goa then do check this video on the left or if you are looking for other details about Goa then do check this video on the right see in my next video Until then keep traveling

videos.transcript: Goa the party capital of the country no doubt it has got great beaches shacks and pubs but did you know that Goa is actually Gold Mine of natural beauty a lot of people visiting Goa don't export Beyond beaches there are so many beautiful places in Goa that are worth the visit from incredible beaches to mesmerizing waterfalls and from beautiful churches to Great forts Goa has a lot to offer in today's video we will talk about best places to visit in North Goa if you're looking for other details like how to reach Goa where to stay total budget and a lot of other informations then do check out my previous video I'll drop a link to that video in the I button or in the description box below without further Ado let's jump into our list of top 20 places to visit in North Goa [Music] in the early 90s parties and Lord musics were banned in Goa that's where foreigners used to do underground parties in arampol if you see it on the map arambol is situated in extreme North and it was a very remote place at that time arambol was a perfect place to host parties without getting into trouble by the local police it used to be the hippie capital of Goa and we find a lot of international tourists especially Russians at this place as of now arumbal is no more secret place but it still has a lot of VP wipes attached to it arambul Beach is filled with so many Shacks if you are hungry then you can eat at one of these Shacks while enjoying the incredible view of the ocean this is a great Beach to spend time with your friends and family but the main reason why arampol is famous is for its Sweetwater Lake it is a 20 minutes walk from the beach to The Sweetwater Lake on the way to the lake you find so many shopping options if you are planning to buy something then this is one of the best places to shop the Sweetwater lake is right next to the beach this is a perfect place to get into the water the depth of the lake is four to five feet which is ideal place to swim even if you don't know swimming if you need to change your clothes then you can use these changing trends at nominal cost it is a good idea to rent a bed for about 100 rupees so that you can chill by the lake especially if you are planning to visit it in the afternoon there are two more hidden spots here one is where you get natural multani Mitte and the other is a banyan tree where a baba sits these two places were banned when we visited if you want to explore them then you can give it a try when you visit arambol [Music] calangute Beach and Baga Beach are next to each other these beaches are one of the busiest beaches in Goa flooded by tourists the only reason you might want to visit kalangut or Baga Beach is for water sports activities water sports are available in many beaches and the prices differ from one place to other the kalangutan Baga Remain the popular places for water sports with reasonable rates you can either do selected water sports or you can go for a complete package I found going for the complete package is much economical the water sports package usually include fire rides jet ski banana ride bumper ride boat ride and parasailing the prices of this package can vary from 1200 Rupees to 2000 rupees based on your bargaining skills and duration of the activity my suggestion is to walk along the beach and inquiry multiple operators I will make a separate video on water sports and other activities you can do in Goa make sure to subscribe to this channel not to miss that [Music] foreign Beach is one of the happening beaches in Goa it has a wide range of sharks pubs and restaurants to choose from the beach in itself is quite small and crowded just like other beaches anjana is yet another great place for water sports with slightly better prices and Service as compared to Baga or kalangut anjuna is famous for its Wednesday flea market it is a good place if you are looking for shopping options there are a lot of popular eateries here that you can try out like Eva Cafe purple martini and Burger Factory I'll cover the places to eat in Goa in another video so stay tuned for that too [Music] wagathar is a popular beach in North Goa next to chapar of Fort there are not many Shack options on this beach but it is a great place if you want to get into the water the best part about wagatar Beach is at sunset this is a perfect place if you want to watch the beautiful sunset next to wagatar Beach is uteran Beach also called The Little wagatar ozaran Beach is relatively less crowded as compared to wagatar there is no proper Pathway to reach the beach you will have to climb down for a couple of minutes to reach the beach do visit ozran Beach early in the morning to have the entire place for yourself [Music] Goa is some beautiful white sandy beaches among which ashwam beach is one of them ashwambis is less crowded so if you're looking to spend peaceful time away from the crazy crowd then this is a place to be this is a hotspot for international Travelers as the crowd is less and the base is beautiful there are limited shacks and restaurants at this place so if you are looking for food then better check eateries in anjana beach ashram beach is an amazing place to watch the sunset as well foreign [Music] filled with so many beautiful churches if you want to visit churches then there can be no better place than old Goa one of the very famous churches in Goa is Basilica of bomb Jesus popularly called the old Goa Church the reason why this church is so popular is because it houses the Mortal remains of Saint Francis Xavier Saint Francis Xavier was a Christian Missionary and his dead body is kept in this church it is a miracle that his body hasn't decayed for the past 400 years a positive bomb Jesus search is say Cathedral the word say means see in Portuguese say Cathedral is considered to be one of the largest churches in Asia just 700 meters from C Cathedral you will find Saint Augustine's Tower this was one of the biggest churches during Portuguese time but it got demolished and only remains can be found here if you are someone who loves to dig into the history then don't miss this place [Music] Immaculate Conception Church of our Lady simply called the Pancham church is one of the most popular churches in Goa no doubt this church is very beautiful but the main reason for its popularity is because of the influence of film industry this church is featured in a lot of movies and as far as I remember the recent one is Aashiqui 2. it was originally a small Chapel where people used to gather for prayers later in 1600 it was converted to a church when Portuguese were doing their religious expansion a lot of improvements were seen since then and what is seen now is a beautiful church with a unique crisscross architecture panjim Church houses goa's second biggest Bell this Bell was originally installed in Augustine's Church but when Augustine church got demolished the Bellows shifted to Aguada and then later to Pancham Church [Music] if you love to explore ruins then visit facade of old song called church the church is called Our Lady of health and it is situated on the banks of juare river the church was originally built in 1566 but due to a fire accident it got destroyed in 1834 and what remains now is only its facade it was declared a national monument in 1937 and since then the facade is preserved in its original form the place is secluded and you will not find any crowd here that means if you are someone who is looking for some calm Place amidst the chaos then you must visit old song called Church [Music] diwara land is goa's largest river Island located near panjim and it is surrounded by Mandaue River there is no connecting bridge to the silent so the only way to reach it is by taking a ferry ride you can take a ferry ride from multiple places depending on your location we took a ferry ride near Old Goa churches a short and sweet five minutes ride would take you to Devar Island you can take your two wheeler car or even Tempo traveler on this Ferry and the surprising thing is that they don't charge a single penny for passengers or two-wheelers there is a minimal charge for four wheelers Dewar island is a very calm Place away from the crazy crowd you usually see in Goa the countryside roads are very Scenic and you get authentic Goan Vibes the moment you land here devara land is famous for its church called Our Lady of Pity Church it is a beautiful place to spend some quality time and soaking the beauty of nature apart from the church there is nothing much to see on this island [Music] next to diwar island is yet another amazing place called charao Island is surrounded by water only on three sites you can reach this island by ferry ride or you can also reach it by road charawaland is famous for its Salim Ali bird sanctuary there is a mangro walk inside the sanctuary which went viral on Instagram reels and since then a lot of tourists come here to see it apart from walking through the mangrove forest you can hop onto one of the boats for bird sighting experience island is famous for feni production if you don't know feni is a local alcohol in Goa made from cashew you will get this drink everywhere in Goa but it is not exported outside the state so if you are in Goa then don't miss to try feni [Music] rice magos is the oldest sport in Goa it was built in 1551 on the narrow stretch of mandavi river by Portuguese the photo was used by Portuguese to defend their territory against marathas during the independence time the fort was used as a prison to lock down the Freedom Fighters you can see the freedom Fighter's name mentioned in this fort even though ricemonger's Fort was such an important part of goa's History it was completely abandoned in 1993. thanks to UK based trust which funded restoration of reismago's fort in 2008. the restoration work was impressively executed by goa's well-known architect Gerard di Kuna there is a gallery Hall where the history of Goa has been displayed from this fort you can get a mesmerizing view of mandavi river and the surrounding places since a lot of people don't know about this place you can expect less crowd here I personally felt this fort is the best fort in entire Goa next to the fort is rice Margo's Church which is also built around the same time [Music] Aguada Fort is a well-preserved fort overlooking the Arabian Sea the photos originally constructed in 1612 by Portuguese to guard against the Dutch the fort is divided into two parts upper Fort and the lower Fort the lower Fort was used as a safe bird to Portuguese ships while the upper Fort was built as a vantage point to serve as a fort and also as a watering station for the ships the word Aguada means Watering Place in Portuguese this fort had a freshwater spring that was used to supply water to the ships the fort is comprised of a moat underground water storage a lighthouse gun powder room and base tins the Lighthouse at the fort is one of the oldest in India it was operational until 1976. next to the fort is the new Lighthouse that you can visit as well the best part about the new Lighthouse is that you can climb all the way to the top climbing the lighthouse might be difficult for elders and kids as the last stretch is quite tricky to climb once you are on the top you can enjoy 360 degree panoramic view apart from this you can also visit Aguada jail which used to be the largest prison in Goa if you want to visit lower Aguada Fort then search synchrome fort on Google Maps it is around three and a half kilometer from upper Aguada fort foreign [Music] movie and if you did you might remember the iconic scene where three friends conclude their trip to Goa and talk about their future plans well that scene was shot in chapter of Fort and it is mainly famous for that reason apart from the connection with the filmy word the fort has a rich history the views you get from this place are truly mesmerizing Chopra Fort was one of the best places to watch Sunset but with the recent implementation of timings the fort closes at 5 PM avoid wasting chaparra fort in the afternoon as there is no shade or place to sit inside the fort [Music] Goa is not just about beaches churches or Fort Goa is filled with some of the prettiest waterfalls in India among which Harvey waterfalls is one of them if you are visiting Goa during monsoon or just after the monsoon then a visit to harvillan waterfalls is a must don't miss visiting Temple located next to this waterfalls apart from the waterfalls you can also visit harvelam caves on the way back you can either visit Amana Bridge or namazga depending on your route Amana bridge is situated towards panjim this Trail bridge is half a kilometer in length and it is totally photogenic on the other hand if you are going towards north Goa you can visit Namaz car it is an open air area built in turko Persian architecture to reach the mosque you'll have to walk through a narrow road for about 5 minutes the place might look abandoned but it is not it is a protected monument and you will find few tourists at this place local Muslims do gather here for prayers during Eid [Music] para road is a Scenic stretch of road guarded by coconut trees on both sides although this place has been featured in so many movies it got popular after days in the ghee this is where Alia Bhatt and Shahrukh Khan would be cycling in one of the songs the stretch is 800 meters long and it is totally insta-worthy you can visit this place to click some amazing photos or shoot some rails keep in mind that this road is not meant to be a tourist spot it is used by locals for their daily commute make sure not to disturb the traffic as the local panchayat has already put up a sign for no parking on this stretch if nuisance continues they might even ban photography on this road I would suggest you to come here early in the morning so that there won't be any traffic or other tourists foreign [Music] then you should head to fontanas in panjim it is probably the most Instagram spot in Goa Fontana's is also called as a Latin quarters and this is a place where Portuguese used to live you will see beautiful and colorful houses in indo-portugal architecture you can walk inside fontanas to find a perfect spot to take photos for your Instagram speaking of Instagram if you're not following us on Instagram then do follow us there I will leave a link to our Instagram profile in the description box below fontanas is best explored on foot so park your vehicle somewhere and start walking through the narrow roads of fontanas there are many spots who are taking photos you can take this screenshot for your reference the food options inside fontanas is limited but they are really good you can visit vivago for authentic go on food Joseph bar to enjoy local drink or Comfort Area 31 January to grab yourself some delicious Bakery items [Music] Goa is some of the most beautiful temples which a lot of people don't know about if you are visiting Goa with family then you can add couple of temples to your itinerary most of the popular temples are located in mardol town in Ponda taluk if you are traveling from panjim side you can take a 20 minutes ferry ride to reach other side of jawari river while there are many temples in this area the most popular one is Sri mangesh Temple dedicated to Lord Shiva it is quite common to see huge crowd here as a lot of locals and tourists visit this Temple regularly apart from mangesh Temple you can also visit mahalasana Temple which is really beautiful another beautiful Temple is the Lakshmi narasimha Temple the highlight of this Temple is its pushkarani the place was so calm and peaceful that I cannot explain it in words [Music] we have seen casinos in a lot of movies but in Goa you can experience one for yourself Goa has the best casinos in India if you're wondering isn't gambling is illegal in India then the answer is yes it's illegal but except for Goa Daman and Sikkim you will find two kinds of casinos in Goa land casinos and cruise casinos 90 percentage of the casinos are located in panjim and you cannot go unnoticed when you are exploring Pancham there are charges for entering casinos and the prices differ from one casino to another with the entry ticket you usually get chips for thousand rupees to play We visited Big Daddy Casino which was good for the price we paid once you enter Casino you'll get access to unlimited alcohol unlimited food live entertainment and playing area roulette under bar Blackjack and poker are some of the popular games here in short it's a different word out there I'm not promoting gambling here but if you want to experience Casino then Goa is the best place to try gambling is quite addictive so visit it just for your experience [Music] if you are one of those who is interested in history architecture and culture then you must visit a museum in Goa there are many museums in Goa among which houses of Goa museum is one of the popular museums it is built in the shape of a boat and that in itself is highlight of this place it is a three-story modern building exhibiting various architectural drawings history of Goa and a few artifacts unlike traditional museums this place doesn't have a lot of vintage items apart from learning a little bit about history and architecture you can take some beautiful photos here next to the museum is Maria Miranda's art gallery for those who don't know about him Mario Miranda was a very famous cartoonist based in Goa the art gallery has very beautiful collection of his works [Music] donopolla is located on the Sea stretch that spans from panjim this place has been featured in so many movies if you remember Ajay devgan's Singham movie it was shot at this place because donopolla is so popular you can expect a lot of crowds here there are some renovation work going on because of which we couldn't go till the Viewpoint from this Viewpoint you get a 360 degree view of Arabian Sea and the surrounding places donopolla is an amazing Place towards the sunset as well let us my list of top 20 places to see in North Goa let me know which is your favorite in the comment section below if you know any other place that deserves to be on this list then do let me know in the comment section as well if you like this video and then hit on that like button if you're new to this channel then hit on the Subscribe button if you haven't watched my previous video of complete guide to Goa then click on this video to see it I'll see you in my next video Until then keep traveling

Sagar we visited Goa for a week and saw famous churches explored hidden waterfalls did water sports activities try trending cafes and chill by the beach in this video we will share the perfect Goa trip to Goa is whether to visit North Goa or South Goa well it completely depends on what are your expectations from Goa Norco is a happening place it has lot of pubs activities and attractions on the other hand South Goa is less crowded it is very calm and peaceful choosing anyone is in fact very difficult but if you are visiting Goa for the first time or you love parties and nightlife or you are with family especially kids then North Korea would be the perfect option for you if you have visited Goa before you like calm places away from the crowd and want to explore the hidden gems of Goa then South Goa would be the best option for you the next question is how many days are required to explore Goa you see Goa is fairly a small straight but there are so many attractions you can visit in Goa if you want to explore Goa thoroughly then you need at least one week you can spend four days in North Goa and another three days in South Goa if you have limited time then you can visit only one part of Goa and next time you can visit the other I have designed this itinerary such that it will be helpful whether you want to visit only North Goa or only South Goa or both let's answer the important question where to stay in Goa you can easily find a lot of accommodation options throughout Goa for all budget categories since we will be exploring both north Goa and South Goa you can book for three nights in North Goa and another three nights in South Goa in North Goa he can stay in anjana kalangut Baga or wagatar all the attractions are convenient to reach from these places and in South Goa you can either stay in Palo Lem or close to palolam like path name or agonda I have mentioned the recommended stays in the description box below you can check it out if you're looking for some good stay options the last question before we jump into our itinerary is how to travel in Goa the public transport in Goa is not that well connected and it's not the most convenient one so I don't recommend it the best way to travel inside Goa is either by renting a two-wheeler renting a four-wheeler or hiring a cab having a two wheeler is the best and the cheapest way to travel around but if you cannot explore on two wheeler for any reason then you can rent a car or go for a cab now that we have answered the basic questions let's jump into the detailed itinerary you can follow foreign reach Goa in the morning if you are planning to rent a vehicle then that is the first thing to do as soon as you reach Goa rentals are easily available near the railway station bus stand or Airport by renting a vehicle here you will save a lot of money on cap for traveling to your hotel but if you have a lot of luggage or not planning to rent a vehicle then you can hire a cab or an auto to reach your hotel alternately you can also take a bus to reach your stay if you are taking the public transport then you may have to change a couple of buses to finally reach your destination once you reach your stay check into your hotel and freshen up today we will be exploring the churches and also visit a casino in the evening casinos have dress code for men shorts and slippers are not allowed make sure you are wearing full pant and shoes for today after good breakfast you can try towards panjim and visit the famous panjim Church it does look amazing in photos you can spend around half an hour here and click some good photos after visiting panjim Church drive towards old Goa churches there are so many churches within a walkable distance in this area you need at least one to two hours to visit all the places here after visiting these churches you can have lunch in a nearby restaurant after lunch you can head towards Saint Augustine's Tower if you're into history and love is Sting ruins then this is a great place after spending around 15 to 20 minutes drive towards divar Island to reach devara land you will have to open to a ferry visit the famous church here called Our Lady of Pity Church make sure to start riding back by 5 PM and return to the mainland now you have to decide whether you want to visit a casino or not if you don't have plan to visit a casino then you can head to Dona Paula and watch the sunset there after that you can head back to your stay in North Goa if you are trying to visit a casino then you can head towards Casino Lane next to Mandaue River the entry ticket can cost anywhere between 2000 Rupees to 3000 rupees per person with entry ticket you get access to casino unlimited food unlimited alcohol and complimentary chips of around thousand rupees you can spend next two to three hours playing games enjoying drinks eating food and watching the live entertainment after a good time at casino you can head back to a stay in North Goa and sleep [Music] get up early and get ready by 8 AM dress up accordingly because we are going to visit the beaches today remember to carry a towel and extra clothes since we will be getting into the water as well head to one of the most trending place called Eva Cafe for breakfast it has stunning decor and breadtaking views it's a perfect spot for capturing Instagram worthy photos although the menu has delicious options be mindful that it is bit pricier after finishing your breakfast right towards arambol Beach once you reach the beach start walking towards arambul Sweetwater Lake it's a one and a half kilometer walk and should take 15 to 20 minutes after reaching arambol Sweetwater Lake you can take refreshing swim in the lake and chill out for some time spend around one to two hours at this place and then start walking back to a rumble Beach there are hundreds of Shacks on this beach if you want to spend some more time here then you can sit at any Shack and have lunch while enjoying the beach wipes if not then head to a restaurant called Garden of dreams for lunch this place has wipes exactly like that of Bali after a good lunch you can either visit chapra Fort or ranjana Beach if you want to do shopping then visit anjana Beach otherwise you can visit chapora fort after visiting either chapra Fort Orange Beach Drive towards ozron Beach for the sunset alternatively you can also visit wagathar Beach for sunset what's the mesmerizing Sunset and head back to his stay the day is not over it freshen up and change to party wear because we are going to a nightclub tonight places like anjana Baga and kalangoot are filled with so many clubs you can visit Tito's Lane calangute Beach or paga Beach for parting if you're staying in anjana like we did then you can visit purple martini after party you can come back to stay and sleep [Music] you may be having a hangover from previous night but still try to wake up early no need to Bath as we are going for water sports activities today wear something comfortable and make sure not to carry any valuable items or bags otherwise while doing water sports activities you will have to deposit them in lockers and they charge insane amount for lockers visit Arjuna Cafe for breakfast after breakfast head towards sanjana Beach Baga Beach or kalangut Beach for water sports activities go for the complete package that includes fire rights jet ski banana right boat ride bumper ride and parasailing after enjoying the water sports head back to your stay and freshen up because we are going to click a lot of photos today wear something colorful and vibrant the first photo spot for today is going to be para road you can click some good photos here or shoot some rails for Instagram after a small photo shoot you can start riding towards houses of Goa Museum this museum is small yet very photogenic you can click some good photos here and learn a little bit about goa's history and architecture the last option for today is the fontanas it is called the Latin quarter and is famous for its colorful houses built in indo-portugal architecture you can park your vehicle and start walking to find the best spot to click your photos if you are feeling hungry then don't miss this bakery called confedera 31 Genero they serve some delicious Bakery items if you have time then visit sharava land in the evening if you are not interested in photos then there is an alternate plan for you you can visit harveyor waterfalls especially if you are visiting in Monsoon or post Monsoon after lunch start driving towards Harvey waterfalls and explore the waterfalls and nearby caves while coming back you can visit namaskar and return to your hotel optionally if you want to party then you can go to a club tonight as well foreign [Music] Fort and later we will move to South Goa if you don't have a plan to visit South Goa then you can catch the written bus train or flight in the evening get ready and check out from your hotel by 10 o'clock if you have got the car then keep all the luggages in the car otherwise you can keep the luggages in the hotel itself the first stop for today is a quarter Fort this fort is very large and will take at least 2 hours to explore after visiting Aguada Fort drive towards Rice mago's Fort you can click some amazing photos here and learn a lot about Ford's history after visiting rice Moguls you can have food at a nearby restaurant called spice Goa this restaurant is famous for seafood and has great Ambience after lunch you can start driving towards your stay in South Goa it is going to be 3 hour long drive to your stay in Palo Lane check into your stay and take rest foreign [Music] we are going to explore the beautiful beaches of South Goa so get up early and dress up with Beach wipes have breakfast at hotel and start riding by nine o'clock reach the first destination of the day kabadrama beach kabadrama beach is less crowded and absolutely beautiful you can chill by the beach or play in the water the beach is clean and water is super clear after spending some quality time at beach start riding towards kabadurama fort there is nothing much to see inside kapoorama Fort except Pebble Beach Pebble Beach is incredible and I would say it is truly a Hidden Gem of Goa you can't get in the water here but you can click so many good photos head to Cape Goa for lunch this place serves delicious food and has great Ambience after this you can start riding towards kakulam Beach this is again one of the quietest beach in Goa super beautiful and worth the visit make sure to start riding towards Kola beach by 4 pm reaching Kola Beach is little difficult especially if you are driving a four-wheeler two wheelers can go till the last point but if you are coming on a car then you will have to walk for around 20 to 30 minutes try to reach Kola beach before 5 PM Cola Beach is undoubtedly one of the most beautiful beaches in Goa you can enjoy the beach Vibes and play in the water Kola beach has lagoons attached to it and kayaking in these lagoons is one of the most popular activities do kayaking and later watch the mesmerizing Sunset after Sunset head back to your stay in palolam if you want to party then you can head to any club in Palo Lim or if you just want to chill by the beach then head to Palo Alam beach oraconda beach and enjoy the food in any Shack [Music] on day 6 we are going to visit waterfalls so carrier towel and extra clothes get up early and get ready before 7 am have breakfast in your hotel or near to your hotel as there are no restaurants on the way after breakfast start riding towards netra Valley Wildlife Sanctuary there are two waterfalls inside the forest mainappi waterfalls and savory waterfalls you can visit either of them or both to reach minapi waterfalls you will have to Trek for around 4 kilometers and this track can take around one and a half hour on the other hand to reach sauri waterfalls you will have to walk less than one kilometer and it takes just 20 to 30 minutes to reach whichever waterfalls you visit don't miss getting into the water and enjoying your time after the waterfalls visit bubbling Lake this place is popular for the mysterious phenomena of bubbles coming from the bottom of the pond start riding back by one o'clock and try to reach kalki Paka beach by 2 pm even though there are no shacks at beach there are plenty of restaurants next to the beach you can have food in one of those restaurants after the lunch spend some time on this beach this is also called The Turtle Beach and if you visit it in the month of March and April you may see the turtle eggs as well after spending a good time at galibaga beach start riding towards palolam beach in palolin Beach you can try kayaking in open ocean and watch the mesmerizing sunset from this beach after the sunset you can go for shopping there are many good restaurants near the beach we tried nerias and it was amazing you can have dinner at this place handed back to yesterday [Music] this is your last day in Goa so for one last time get up early in the morning and get ready you can have breakfast at just Cafe today we will be visiting butterfly Beach there are two ways to reach butterfly Beach you can either take a boat ride or you can Trek to this beach if you are choosing the first option then head to palolin beach or path name Beach and open to one of the boats the boat tour will take around one and a half hour and it will show you Monkey Island Turtle Rock Honeymoon Beach and Butterfly Beach if you are choosing the second option which is hiking then you will have to hike anywhere between 30 minutes to 60 Minutes depending on whether you are on a two wheeler or four wheeler after you are done with a good day at butterfly Beach head back to your hotel and check out if you have time then you can explore the famous temples of Goa like mangesh Temple mahalasana Temple and Lakshmi narasimha Temple or if you like to explore the ruins then you can visit the facade of old song called Church in the evening return your rented vehicle and head to the bus stand railway station or airport however you are planning to travel back [Music] if you want to extend your trip by one more day or didn't like the itinerary of any day then you can replace it by those other waterfalls you can start from your hotel as early as possible and reach a place called kulam Park your vehicle here and buy the ticket for the Jeep ride to dusagar waterfalls you can enjoy the off-road Jeep ride do a little bit of walking and finally reach the waterfalls the entire activity can take up to 4 hours while returning from do Sagar stop at salulim Dam this is a must visit especially during monsoon time if you are lucky then you can get this kind of view after spending a good time at salalim Dam you can return to your stay to summarize these are the places you can visit each day this itinerary is jam-packed and if you are finding it hard to cover places in time or want to keep it chilled then you can skip a few places places shown in blue are a must visit places shown in yellow are a good to visit and places shown in Gray can be skipped if you're running out of time I hope this video is helpful for you don't forget to check the deal in the description box below if you have any question about planning a trip to Goa then don't forget to check it out you can click on the videos shown on the screen to watch them I'll see you in those videos thanks for watching
